content: This document defines comprehensive quality metrics and measurement criteria for creative phases, ensuring that design decisions meet required standards and are properly documented.
Documentation Quality requires a clear problem statement, well-defined objectives, a comprehensive requirements list, proper formatting and structure, and cross-references to related documents.
Decision Coverage requires that all required decisions are identified, each decision point is documented, dependencies are mapped, impact analysis is included, and future considerations are noted.
Option Analysis requires that multiple options are considered, pros/cons are documented, technical feasibility is assessed, resource requirements are estimated, and risk factors are identified.

Impact Assessment requires that system impact is documented, performance implications are assessed, security considerations are addressed, maintenance impact is evaluated, and cost implications are analyzed.
The Verification Score requires that requirements are traced, constraints are validated, test scenarios are defined, review feedback is incorporated, and final verification is completed.
The minimum required score for the creative phase quality assessment is 40 out of 50 (80%).
For scores below the threshold, improvements should be made to documentation quality, decision coverage, option analysis, impact assessment, and verification.

A pre-review verification checklist must be completed, ensuring all sections are scored, calculations are verified, supporting evidence is attached, improvement areas are identified, and review feedback is incorporated.

A final metrics verification must confirm that the minimum score is achieved, all categories have passed, documentation is complete, improvements have been addressed, and final approval has been obtained.
